Do you ever argue with your friends? What about?
What is the difference between a row and a normal argument? Have you ever had a row with someone?
Have you ever been involved in a court case or legal proceedings?
What are some everyday situations in which you're likely to complain? Are you quick to complain about things or do you try to put up with things until a situation becomes unbearable?
questions.paraphrased: Do you tend to getting angry when you complain or are you usually calm and controlled?

questions.original: Describe a time you have complained strongly about something. Did it help the situation?
questions.paraphrased: At any point in your life, have you complained about food in a restaurant? What was the issue?

questions.original: Describe a time someone has complained strongly to you about a situation you've caused. Do you feel it was justified and how did you react?
questions.paraphrased: Have you ever complained about rude service? Do you think service in your home country usually good or bad?

questions.original: Are there times when you've complained about something but regretted it later?
questions.paraphrased: Is there anything that you regularly get criticised for? Do you believe the criticism is fair?

questions.original: Are you prone to getting angry when you complain or are you usually calm and controlled?
Do you ever offer an apology to someone just to keep the peace, even when you don't believe that you've done anything wrong? How do you feel afterward?
Have you ever complained about food in a restaurant? What was the issue?
Have you ever complained about rude service? Do you think service in your country usually good or bad?
Do you often complain to your friends or family?
Do you consider yourself to be a critical person? Are you quick to judge others?
What are some things that tend to attract your criticism?
Is there anything that you regularly get criticised for? Do you think the criticism is fair?
Do you agree that offering constructive criticism is a better approach than emphasizing the negatives when encouraging people to change?
What aspects of yourself are you most critical about?
Do you enjoy studying? If so what do you like about it?
questions.paraphrased: Would you rather arts and humanities based subjects or mathematics and the sciences?

questions.original: What courses are you currently studying? Why are you studying them?
questions.paraphrased: From your perspective how important is education to success in life?

questions.original: Did you enjoy your school years? Why or why not?
questions.paraphrased: Does a good education usually lead to a good job in your home country?

questions.original: Do you consider yourself to be an academic person?
questions.paraphrased: Do you believe school and university courses are too theoretical? Should young people be learning more practical, 'real world' skills such as personal finance and household management?

questions.original: Do you prefer arts and humanities based subjects or mathematics and the sciences?
In your opinion how important is education to success in life?
Does a good education usually lead to a good job in your country?
Do you know anyone who has been successful in their careers without a proper education?
Do you know anyone who is highly qualified but has found it hard to get a job?
Do you think school and university courses are too theoretical? Should young people be learning more practical, 'real world' skills such as personal finance and household management?
What age do you think teenagers should be allowed to drop out of school?
Would you consider working in the higher education field as an academic?
What is your main purpose for learning English?
questions.paraphrased: Have you got a broad English vocabulary?

questions.original: When did you start learning English?
questions.paraphrased: Have you got any special tricks to help you to learn?

questions.original: Do you ever have to use English as part of your day to day life?
Do you enjoy learning English or is it a chore?
What aspect of English is most difficult to master?
What is your strongest point in English?
What is your weakest point in English?
Do you have a broad English vocabulary?
Do you find grammar difficult?
Are you usually confident speaking in English? How can you become confident?
Is living abroad the best way to master a language?
Do you have any special tricks to help you to learn?
Do you consider yourself to be an intelligent person?
questions.paraphrased: Do you feel that academic success usually reflects intelligence?

questions.original: Are you especially good at any particular subject or skill?
questions.paraphrased: Do you believe working hard or intelligence is more importance to success in life?

questions.original: How were your grades at school? Did you excel at certain subjects?
questions.paraphrased: What is the difference between intelligence and wisdom? Do you believe they are related?

questions.original: Who is the most intelligent person you know? How are they smart?
questions.paraphrased: Do you believe intelligence and happiness are related or not?

questions.original: Do you think that academic success usually reflects intelligence?
questions.paraphrased: Do you believe it is better to have a high IQ or to be 'street smart'?

questions.original: Do you think working hard or intelligence is more importance to success in life?
questions.paraphrased: Do you believe IQ tests are a good way to measure a person's intelligence or do they tend to favour people who are good at maths?

questions.original: What is the difference between intelligence and wisdom? Do you think they are related?
Do you think intelligence and happiness are related or not?
Do you consider yourself to be 'street smart'?
Do you think it is better to have a high IQ or to be 'street smart'?
Do you think IQ tests are a good way to measure a person's intelligence or do they tend to favour people who are good at maths?

Do you agree with the theory of multiple intelligences? (That is, intelligence is not dominated by a single general ability but is formed by different types of intelligence, such as visual-spatial ability, musical sensitivity and interpersonal skills.)
Would you prefer to have good looks or a high IQ?
